payload.intent: This iteration establishes the initial structural skeleton of the system specification, organizing it into clearly numbered sections that align with the scoring rubric. It introduces a glossary for domain terminology, defines component responsibilities with single-sentence summaries, and provides placeholder examples and scoped open items. This scaffolding ensures the judge can evaluate cross-references, completeness, and formatting consistency before deeper content refinement begins.

payload.critique: The artifact fails rubric criteria 1, 2, and 3: §4 component first sentences do not explicitly name a single responsibility, the cross-reference to non-existent §9.2 is broken, and domain jargon is defined in §6 after already being used throughout the document. Immediately move the glossary to precede §1 (or define terms inline on first use), fix all cross-references to point only to existing sections, and rewrite each §4 component’s opening sentence to explicitly declare its single responsibility.
